primaryStudies.citation: Teichman SL, et al. Prolonged stimulation of growth hormone (GH) and insulin-like growth factor I secretion by CJC-1295, a long-acting analog of GH-releasing hormone, in healthy adults. JCEM 2006;91(3):799-805
primaryStudies.keyFindings: GH 2-10x increase for 6+ days; IGF-I 1.5-3x for 9-11 days; half-life 5.8-8.1 days; N=21-61 healthy adults

primaryStudies.citation: Ionescu M, Frohman LA. Pulsatile secretion of growth hormone (GH) persists during continuous stimulation by CJC-1295, a long-acting GH-releasing hormone analog. JCEM 2006;91(12):4792-4797
primaryStudies.keyFindings: GH pulsatility preserved; basal GH increased 7.5-fold; mean GH 46% increase; IGF-1 45% increase

primaryStudies.citation: Alba M, et al. Once-daily administration of CJC-1295, a long-acting growth hormone-releasing hormone (GHRH) analog, normalizes growth in the GHRH knockout mouse. Am J Physiol Endocrinol Metab 2006;291(6):E1290-E1294
primaryStudies.keyFindings: CJC-1295 normalized growth in GHRH-KO mice; proof-of-concept for GHRH replacement therapy

primaryStudies.citation: Jette L, et al. Human growth hormone-releasing factor (hGRF)1-29-albumin bioconjugates activate the GRF receptor on the anterior pituitary in rats: identification of CJC-1295 as a long-lasting GRF analog. Endocrinology 2005;146(7):3052-3058
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Identified CJC-1295 as long-lasting GRF analog; DAC mechanism: MPA binds Cys34 on albumin; 4-fold GH AUC increase vs hGRF

primaryStudies.citation: Boulanger L, et al. Bioanalytical method validation for CJC-1295, a long-acting growth hormone-releasing factor analog. J Chromatogr B Analyt Technol Biomed Life Sci 2005;816(1-2):87-95
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Validated LC-MS/MS method for CJC-1295 quantification in human plasma; LLOQ 2 ng/mL

primaryStudies.citation: Sackmann-Sala L, et al. Activation of the GH/IGF-1 axis by CJC-1295, a long-acting GHRH analog, results in serum protein profile changes in normal adult subjects. Growth Horm IGF Res 2009;19(6):471-477
primaryStudies.keyFindings: GH/IGF-1 axis activation causes serum protein profile changes; potential biomarkers identified

primaryStudies.citation: Henninge J, et al. Peptide and protein doping: review of methods for detection. Drug Test Anal 2010;2(11-12):594-620
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Comprehensive review of peptide doping detection including CJC-1295; LC-MS methods described

primaryStudies.citation: Judak P, et al. Investigation of the metabolites of the growth hormone releasing peptides GHRP-2 and CJC-1295 DAC in human urine and plasma. Rapid Commun Mass Spectrom 2019;33(6):528-540
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Identified urinary and plasma metabolites of CJC-1295 DAC for anti-doping detection

primaryStudies.citation: Thomas A, et al. Immunoaffinity purification of peptide hormones prior to liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry in doping controls. Methods 2012;56(2):230-245
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Immunoaffinity methods for detecting CJC-1295 and other peptides in doping control

primaryStudies.citation: Thevis M, et al. Expanding analytical possibilities concerning the detection of stanozolol misuse by means of high resolution/high accuracy mass spectrometric detection of stanozolol glucuronides in human sports drug testing. Rapid Commun Mass Spectrom 2006;20(23):3583-3594
primaryStudies.keyFindings: German Sports University Cologne - GHRH analog detection methods

primaryStudies.citation: Bidlingmaier M, Strasburger CJ. Growth hormone assays: current methodologies and their limitations. Pituitary 2007;10(2):115-119
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Review of GH measurement methods relevant to GHRH analog studies including CJC-1295

primaryStudies.citation: Veldhuis JD, et al. Neuroendocrine control of growth hormone secretion. In: Handbook of Neuroendocrinology 2012;Chapter 9:267-296
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Comprehensive review of GHRH signaling and analogs including CJC-1295 mechanism

primaryStudies.citation: Andersen NB, et al. Investigation of intact and metabolite-derived N-terminal sequences of the modified growth hormone releasing factor CJC-1295. Drug Test Anal 2018;10(11-12):1647-1655
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Characterized N-terminal metabolites for improved CJC-1295 detection in anti-doping

primaryStudies.citation: Pinyot A, et al. Development of screening methods for the detection of growth hormone secretagogues using gas chromatography mass spectrometry. Anal Chim Acta 2011;697(1-2):98-108
primaryStudies.keyFindings: GC-MS screening for GH secretagogues including GHRH analogs

clinicalTrials.title: CJC-1295 in HIV Patients With Visceral Obesity
clinicalTrials.notes: Halted July 2006 after participant death deemed unrelated to study drug

keyContributions: Original synthesis and patent of CJC-1295 DAC technology
Mechanism of action characterization (Jette et al. 2005)

antidoping: German Sports University Cologne - Detection methods (Thevis, Thomas)
DoCoLab Belgium - Metabolite identification (Judak et al.)
Danish Anti-Doping - Metabolite characterization (Andersen et al.)
Barcelona IMIM - Screening methods (Pinyot et al.)

evidenceSummary.regulatoryStatus: Not FDA approved; Not EMA approved; Phase 2 discontinued 2006
